chapter_context.summary: Presents a grand poetic lamentation over Tyre, described as a glorious merchant ship built of premium timbers, laden with fine wares from all over the ancient world, which is shattered in the heart of the seas by the east wind.

outline.text: Tyre as a Glorious, Perfectly Constructed Merchant Ship
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Tyre as a Glorious, Perfectly Constructed Merchant Ship (27:1-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The Extensive Registry of Tyre's Trade with All Nations
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Extensive Registry of Tyre's Trade with All Nations (27:12-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The Devastating Shipwreck of Tyre in the Heart of the Seas
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Devastating Shipwreck of Tyre in the Heart of the Seas (27:26-36), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Pagan Nations
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against pagan nations, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 27, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
